visual_checks: Look for any timed interactions such as countdown timers, auto-advancing content, or time-limited forms
Check if any content or functionality is gated by a timer or deadline
Look for auto-playing slideshows, carousels, or content rotators with timed transitions
Check for session timeouts, quiz timers, or auction countdowns
Verify that no interactive process requires completion within a time limit
Look for real-time events (live auctions, live streams) where timing may be inherent and essential
pass_conditions: No timing constraints are imposed on any user interaction or content consumption
Users can take as long as needed to complete any task or process on the page
Any timed elements are non-essential and do not affect the user's ability to access content or complete tasks
If timing exists, it is part of a real-time event where timing is an essential and unavoidable aspect of the activity
fail_conditions: A time limit is imposed on completing a form, quiz, or interactive process where timing is not essential
Content auto-advances or expires after a set period without user control
A session timeout logs the user out without timing being essential to the activity
Auto-playing carousels or slideshows advance on a timer with no way to stop or control the timing
Timed assessments impose limits where the subject matter does not require real-time performance
na_conditions: Page contains no timed interactions, auto-advancing content, or time-limited processes
All timed elements on the page are part of inherently real-time events (e.g., live auction, live broadcast) where removing timing would fundamentally change the activity
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Page content failed to load so timing behaviors cannot be evaluated
common_mistakes: Imposing time limits on forms or surveys where timing serves no essential purpose
Auto-advancing carousels that move content away before users finish reading
Quiz or test timers where the knowledge being assessed does not require timed performance
Session timeouts treated as necessary when the activity itself has no real-time requirement
Promotional banners or notifications that disappear after a set time without user control
Assuming that time limits are acceptable as long as they are generous

examples.pass: An online course quiz allows users unlimited time to answer questions, with no countdown timer, because the knowledge assessment does not depend on speed
examples.fail: A feedback survey imposes a 10-minute time limit to complete all questions, even though there is no essential reason for the time constraint
examples.partial: Most forms and interactions have no time limits, but an auto-advancing product carousel moves slides every 5 seconds with no pause or stop control

plain_meaning: AAA version of 2.2.1: timing must not be essential to the activity at all, except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events. The intent is complete removal of time pressure from interactive tasks.
scope_applies_to: Timed tests, quizzes, or tasks imposed by the content
Session timeouts that interrupt user activity
Any interactive activity with an imposed deadline
scope_does_not_apply_to: Non-interactive synchronized media (videos, audio with captions)
Pages with no time limits at all
borderline_calibration: If no imposed timing exists, this SC is N/A.
Real-time events and synchronized media playback are categorically exempt.
Any interactive deadline set by the content that is not essential fails this SC even if 2.2.1 passes with adjustment options.
